cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user provided a passage about a study on self-talk and then a question asking about the students' performance in the experiment. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the key points from the passage. The study had 143 students who were asked to write down advice to themselves using either first-person ("I") or second-person ("You"). The tasks involved puzzles and planning to exercise more. The results showed that students using the second-person completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Also, when it came to exercising, they planned to do more and had a better attitude compared to the first-person group. The question is asking how the students performed in the experiment. The options are: A) First-person had better attitude. B) Those who talked to themselves completed most puzzles. C) Second-person writing were better. D) First-person more positive. From the passage, the students who wrote in the second-person (C) did better in completing puzzles and had a more positive attitude. The other options don't match the findings. A says first-person had better attitude, which contradicts the study. B mentions
